name: Zoom & Pan
description: Ken Burns style image motion: every render draws a slightly different crop, so consecutive renders form a smooth zoom and pan. Only useful on displays that can render many times per second (HDMI, LCD, web) - e-ink panels refresh far too slowly for this.

fields.name: motion
fields.label: Motion
fields.hint: kenBurns combines zooming with a focal point that drifts towards a new corner every cycle.

fields.name: zoomStart
fields.label: Start zoom
fields.hint: Zoom level at the start of a cycle. 1.0 shows the largest crop of the image that covers the canvas.

fields.name: zoomEnd
fields.label: End zoom
fields.hint: Zoom level at the end of a cycle. Pan motions keep this zoom for the whole cycle.

fields.name: durationSeconds
fields.label: Cycle duration (seconds)
fields.hint: Time for one full zoom cycle.

fields.name: anchor
fields.label: Anchor
fields.hint: Part of the image the zoom stays locked on. Random picks a new deterministic spot every cycle.

fields.name: phaseStateKey
fields.label: Phase state key
fields.hint: Enter a state key to persist the animation phase so scene re-activations continue smoothly.
